input_dict.agent_role: Summarize first-hop evidence and expose the bridge entity, missing fact, or retrieval direction needed for the next hop.
input_dict.question: Who wrote the debut song on the debut album of the artist who also released the album "I'm Still Here"?
input_dict.current_output:
Evidence found:
- Evans Blue — [1]: '"Cold (But I'm Still Here)" is the first single'
- Agnes — [2]: 'The track was written by Jörgen Elofsson'
- Foster & Lloyd — [3]: '"Crazy Over You" is a debut song'
- Esmée Denters — [4]: '"Outta Here" is the title song of the debut studio album'
- Gabriela (singer) — [5]: 'Her self-titled debut album was released in 2001;'
- Sammy Kershaw — [6]: 'from his debut album, "Don't Go Near the Water".'
- Melanie Martinez — [7]: 'this song is also the first official song Melanie Martinez wrote.'

Ambiguities:
- The phrase "I'm Still Here" appears as part of the song title "Cold (But I'm Still Here)" (passage [1]) but no passage shows an album titled "I'm Still Here".
- Multiple "debut song" examples exist (e.g., Foster & Lloyd, Melanie Martinez) but none are linked to an album titled "I'm Still Here".

Bridge / missing fact:
- Missing identity of the artist who released an album titled "I'm Still Here".
- Missing that artist's debut album title and which track is the debut song on that debut album.
- Missing the songwriter credit for that debut song.

Retrieval direction:
- Open "I'm Still Here (album)" (Wikipedia page) or "I'm Still Here (disambiguation)" to identify the album's artist.
- Run the query: '"I'm Still Here" album artist' (search exact phrase).
- After identifying the artist, open that artist's page and their debut album page (e.g., '"[Artist name]" discography' or '"[Artist name]" debut album').
- Also check "Cold (But I'm Still Here)" (passage [1]) to avoid confusing the song title with an album title.
Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets above.

upstream_context.question: Who wrote the debut song on the debut album of the artist who also released the album "I'm Still Here"?
upstream_context.hop1_query: Who wrote the debut song on the debut album of the artist who also released the album "I'm Still Here"?
hop1_docs: Cold (But I'm Still Here) | "Cold (But I'm Still Here)" is the first single off Evans Blue's debut album, "The Melody and the Energetic Nature of Volume".  The song was released December 13, 2005, two months prior to the album release, and garnered frequent radio play in anticipation of the upcoming album.  Evans Blue's official MySpace explains the meaning of this song as well as others by the group.  A music video was also produced for "Cold (But I'm Still Here)."  The song currently has over three million plays on MySpace.

Kick Back Relax | "Kick Back Relax" is a pop song recorded by Swedish singer and Idol winner Agnes taken from her second album "Stronger".  The track was written by Jörgen Elofsson who also wrote her debut single "Right Here Right Now".  It was released as the album's first single in September 2006.

Crazy Over You | "Crazy Over You" is a debut song recorded by American country music duo Foster & Lloyd, who also wrote the song.  It was released in May 1987 as the first single from their self-titled debut album.  It was their most successful single, peaking at #4 on the "Billboard" Hot Country Songs chart in 1987.  The song was simultaneously recorded and released by Ricky Van Shelton on his debut album, "Wild-Eyed Dream".

Outta Here (song) | "Outta Here" is the title song of the debut studio album by Dutch R&B singer-songwriter Esmée Denters.  It was released as the debut single both from the artist and the album in the Netherlands on 14 April 2009, 27 April in New Zealand followed by 16 August in the UK.

Gabriela (singer) | Her self-titled debut album was released in 2001; it was produced by Vehnee Saturno, who also wrote eight of the album's ten songs.  Another of the songs was a rendition of Bryan Adams's "Please Forgive Me".  Her second album, "In the Interim", was released in 2004.

Anywhere but Here (song) | "Anywhere but Here" is the title of the fourth single released by American country music artist Sammy Kershaw.  It was co-written by Bob DiPiero and John Scott Sherrill with Buddy Cannon, who also produced it.  It was released in September 1992 as the final single from his debut album, "Don't Go Near the Water".  It peaked at #10 on the "Billboard" Hot Country Songs chart and #17 on the Canadian "RPM" country singles chart.

Dollhouse (Melanie Martinez song) | "Dollhouse" is the debut single by American recording artist Melanie Martinez.  The song was featured on Martinez's debut EP "Dollhouse" (2014), where it was released as the EP's lead single on February 9, 2014.  "Dollhouse" was later featured on Martinez's debut studio album, "Cry Baby" (2015).  Lyrically, "Dollhouse" is about a dysfunctional family, who, according to Martinez, is "hiding being a perfect plastic facade".  Martinez has also stated that "Dollhouse" is a metaphor for how people view celebrities and their seemingly perfect public lives.  Dollhouse is also the second song of Cry Baby, and this song is also the first official song Melanie Martinez wrote.

- "Cold (But I'm Still Here)": song is the first single from Evans Blue's debut album (contains phrase "I'm Still Here" only as part of a song title).
- "Kick Back Relax" (Agnes): notes Jörgen Elofsson wrote Agnes's debut single "Right Here Right Now" (songwriter credit example for an artist with a similarly phrased hit).
- "Crazy Over You" (Foster & Lloyd): explicitly a debut song written by the duo (example of artist-written debut song).
- "Outta Here" (Esmée Denters): title/debut single from the artist's debut album (example of a debut single associated with a debut album).
- "Gabriela (singer)": mentions details about a 2001 self-titled debut album and its songwriter/producer involvement.
- "Anywhere but Here" (Sammy Kershaw): mentions a song released from a debut album (example of linking a single to a debut album).
- "Dollhouse" (Melanie Martinez): debut single and noted as the first official song she wrote.

Ambiguity / gap:
- None of the retrieved passages identify an album titled exactly "I'm Still Here". The only hit containing that phrase is a song title ("Cold (But I'm Still Here)")—this may be a red herring.

Bridge / missing facts needed for the next hop (do not assume these facts):
- The identity of the artist who released an album titled "I'm Still Here".
- That artist's debut album title and which track on it is considered the debut song.
- The songwriter credit for that debut song.

1) Open "I'm Still Here (album)" or "I'm Still Here (disambiguation)" (Wikipedia) to identify the album's artist.
2) If the album page is unavailable, run an exact-search: "\"I'm Still Here\" album artist" and open the artist's main page.
3) Once the artist is identified, open that artist's discography or the page for their debut album to locate the debut-song track and its songwriter credit.
4) Also retrieve the listed missing-support pages provided at this boundary as targets (do not assume their contents): "Ten Thousand Angels (song)" and "Mindy McCready".
5) Re-check "Cold (But I'm Still Here)" to avoid conflating a song title containing the phrase with an album titled "I'm Still Here".

Final note: no second-hop facts or the answer are asserted here; next agent should fetch the album/artist pages and the two missing-support targets to locate the songwriter credit for the specified debut song.",
  "rationale": "The first-hop documents include several examples linking debut singles to debut albums and a few songwriter attributions (e.g., Jörgen Elofsson for Agnes's debut single, Foster & Lloyd wrote their debut song, Melanie Martinez wrote her first official song).
